hadiths.ar:
583  -  وَّ رُّوِى ه ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمُّ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ كُّل َّ 
ي َّوْمٍ إ َّن ْزَّإرِهَّا وَّ ق ُّح  َّارِهَّا ق َّاجْذ َّرُّوإ وَّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّوْلُّ  إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝  وَّ ق ُّلِ إِعْمَّلُّوإ ق َّسَّي َّزَّى إَّللّٰهُّ   عَّمَّلَّكُّمْ وَّ رَّسُّولُّهُّ وَّ 
إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ُّون َّ  ۝»   »
hadiths.en:
The Prophet, said: "My life is good for you, and my death is good for you."  
They asked: "O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, how is that?" 
He {saws} said: "As for my life, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, says:  
'And Allah {SWT} would not punish them while you are among them' (Surah Al-Anfal 8:33) 
As for my separation from you (in death), your deeds are presented to me every day; 
whatever I find of good, I increase my supplication to Allah {SWT} for you, and whatever I 
find of bad, I seek forgiveness from Allah {SWT} for you."  
They said: "Even after you have become dust (in your grave), O’ Messenger of Allah 
{saws}?"  
He {saws} replied: "No! Verily, Allah {SWT}, Exalted, has forbidden the earth from 
consuming our flesh”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 191 • Al-Wafi, Part 3, p. 546 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 582
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٢

hadiths.ar:
584  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّصْلُّوب ِ ي ُّصِي ن ُّهُّ عَّذ َّإب ُّ إَّلْق َّن ْزِ ق َّق َّال 
ق َّي ُّوجِى  إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ إ ِلَّى إَّلْهَّوَّإءِ ق َّي َّص ْع َّطُّهُّ إ َّس َّذ َّ مِن ْ ص َّع ْطَّه ِ إَّلْق َّن ْزِ .
hadiths.en:
It is reported: "The deeds of the servants are presented to the Messenger of 
Allah {saws} and to the Imams {a.s} every day, both the good and the evil ones.  
So beware! This is the meaning of Allah’s {SWT} statement: 'And say: Work; so Allah {SWT} 
will see your deeds, and [so will] His Messenger and the believers'  (Surah At-Tawbah 
9:105)" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 191 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 16, p. 109 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 583
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٣

hadiths.ar:
585  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّم َّار  إَّلس َّان َّاطِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّه ق َّال 
ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ .   
وذ كز هذ إ ق ى  جذي ث  طوي ل ي صف  ق ي ه ع سل إلمي ث .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the one who is 
crucified (and thus does not have a burial in the ground) and whether they are subject 
to punishment of the grave.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Indeed, the Lord {AZJ} of the earth is the Lord {AZJ} of the air, and Allah {SWT}, 
the  Majestic, commands the air to press upon him more intensely than the squeezing 
of the grave." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 584
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٤

hadiths.en:
Ammar al-Sabati narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}: 
Imam {a.s} said: “If you wash the head and beard of the deceased with khatmi (a type of 
plant-based soap), there is no issue."  
[AL SADUQ] 
He mentioned this in a long narration describing the washing of the deceased.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 192 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 485 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 585
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٥

hadiths.ar:
587   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ي َّمِي ن اً وَّ س ِمَّالأً ي َّص ْن ِطُّهُّ ن ِزِج ْلَّي ْك َّ كَّى ْ لأَّ ي َّسْق ُّظَّ لِوَّج ْهِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"The washing (ghusl) of the deceased is like the washing of one in a state of major ritual 
impurity (ghusl al -janabah). If the deceased has a lot of hair, then pour water over it 
three times." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 192 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 486 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 586
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٦

hadiths.ar:
588  -  وَّ إ ِن  َّ رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ مَّس َّى ج َّلْف َّ ج َّن َّار َّة ِ رَّج ُّلٍ مِن َّ إَّلْأ َّن ْصَّارِ ق َّق ِي لَّ لَّهُّ إ َّ لأَّ ي َّزْكَّث ُّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ  
إَّلل َّهِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"There is no issue if you place the deceased between your legs and stand over him to 
wash him. When you turn him to the right or left, hold him firmly with your legs so that 
he does not fall on his face”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 192 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 447 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 206 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 327 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 543 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 587
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٧

hadiths.ar:
589   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى   إ ج ِزِ جَّذِي ث ٍ: ي َّذ ْكُّزُّ ق ِي هِ ع ُّسْلَّ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ 
ج ِق ْث َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّط ْهَّزَّ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّن ْح ِزَّي ْن ِ س َّى ْء  ق َّلاَّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّصَّي ِّزَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق ُّطْن اً وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّح َّف ْ ق َّلاَّ ي َّ ح ْعَّلْ ق ِي هِ س َّي ْن اً .
hadiths.en:
Messenger of Allah {saws} walked behind the funeral procession of a man from 
Ansar.  
He was asked: "O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, would you not ride?"  
He {saws} replied: "I dislike riding while the angels are walking." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 588
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٨

hadiths.ar: 590 - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى  إ ج ِزِ جَّذِي ث ٍ طَّوِي لٍ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: at the end of a narration 
discussing the washing of the deceased:  
"Be careful not to stuff anything into the ears. However, if you fear that something may 
come out from the nostrils, there is no issue with placing cotton there. But if you do 
not fear anything coming out, do not place anything in them”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 192 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 38 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 589
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٩

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said at the end of a long narration 
describing the washing of the deceased: "Do not interlace (clean) the fingernails”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 192 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 486 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 590
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٩٠

hadiths.ar:
592  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
س َّى ْءٍ ي ُّصْن َّغُّ ن ِهِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ كُّق ِّن َّ وَّ وُّص ِغَّ عَّلَّى إَّلس َّزِي زِ وَّ جُّمِلَّ عَّلَّى إ َّعْن َّاق ِ إَّلزِّج َّالِ عَّاذَّب ِ إَّلز ُّوحُّ إ ِل ي ْهِ وَّ ذَّج َّلَّث ْ  ق ِي هِ ق َّي ُّمَّذ ُّ لَّهُّ 
ق ِى  ن َّصَّزِةِ ق َّي َّن ْط ُّزُّ إ ِلَّى مَّوْص ِعِهِ مِن َّ إَّلْح َّن  َّه ِ إ َّوْ مِن َّ إَّلن  َّارِ ق َّي ُّن َّاذِى  ن ِا َّعْلَّى صَّوْي ِهِ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ مِن ْ إ َّ هْلِ إَّلْح َّن  َّه ِ عَّح ِّلُّون ِى  عَّح ِّلُّون ِى   
وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ مِن ْ إ َّهْلِ إَّلن  َّارِ رُّذ ُّون ِى  رُّذ ُّون ِى  وَّ هُّوَّ ي َّعْ لَّمُّ كُّل َّ س َّى ْءٍ ي ُّصْن َّغُّ ن ِهِ وَّ ي َّسْمَّغُّ إَّلْكَّلاَّمَّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "When one of you dies, lay him down facing the Qibla, 
and likewise, when he is washed, let the place of washing be dug facing the Qibla."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 591
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٩١

hadiths.ar:
593 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ق َّذِمَّث ِ إَّلز ُّوحُّ عَّلَّى إَّلْأ َّرْوَّإحِ ي َّق ُّولُّ ذَّعُّوهَّا ق َّق َّذْ إ َّق ْلَّي َّث ْ مِن ْ هَّوْلٍ عَّط ِي مٍ ي ُّم َّ ي َّسْا َّلُّون َّهَّا مَّا ق َّعَّل   ق ُّلاَّن   وَّ مَّا ق َّعَّ لَّ ق ُّلاَّن   
ق َّا ِن ْ ق َّالَّث ْ لَّهُّمْ ي َّزَّكْي ُّهُّ جَّي  اً إِرْي َّح َّوْةُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ ق َّالَّث ْ لَّهُّمْ ق َّذْ هَّلَّك َّ ق َّالُّوإ هَّوَّى هَّوَّى .
hadiths.en:
Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "When the soul is taken, it hovers above the body, 
whether the person is a believer or otherwise, watching everything that is done to it.  
When the body is shrouded and placed on the bier and carried on the shoulders of 
men, the soul returns to it and enters it, and its sight is extended to see its place in 
Paradise or Hellfire.  
It then calls out with a loud voice: If it is from the people of Paradise, it says, 'Hasten 
me, hasten me!' And if it is from the people of Hellfire, it says, 'Take me back, take me 
back!' It is aware of everything done to it and hears the words spoken."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 193 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 610 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 
58, p. 50 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 592
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٩٢

hadiths.ar:
594  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «وي َّعَّالَّىإ َّوْجَّى إ ِلَّى مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن ْ إ َّج ْزِح ْ 
عِط َّامَّ ي ُّوسُّف َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ مِن ْ مِصْزَّ وَّ وَّعَّذَّةُّ طُّلُّوعَّ إَّلْق َّمَّزِ ق َّا َّن ْطَّا َّ طُّلُّوعُّ إَّلْق َّمَّزِ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّسَّا َّ لَّ عَّم َّن ْ ي َّعْلَّمُّ مَّوْص ِعَّهُّ ق َّق ِي لَّ  
لَّهُّ هَّاهُّن َّا عَّح ُّور     ي َّعْلَّمُّ عِلْمَّهُّ ق َّن َّعَّث َّ إ ِلَّي ْهَّا ق َّا ُّي ِى َّ ن ِعَّح ُّور ٍ مُّق ْعَّذَّة ٍ عَّمْي َّاءَّ ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّعْزِق ِي ن َّ ق َّن ْزَّ ي ُّوسُّف َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّ لاَّمُّ ق َّالَّث ْ  
ن َّعَّمْ ق َّالَّ ق َّا َّج ْن ِزِي ن ِى  ن ِمَّوْص ِعِهِ ق َّالَّث ْ لأَّ إ َّق ْ عَّلُّ جَّي  َّى ي ُّعْطِي َّن ِى  ج ِصَّالأً ي ُّطْلِق َّ رِج ْلَّى  َّ وَّ ي ُّعِي ذَّ إ ِلَّى  َّ ن َّصَّزِى  وَّ ي َّزُّذ َّ إ ِلَّى  َّ 
س َّن َّان ِى  وَّ ي َّح ْعَّلَّن ِى  مَّعَّك َّ ق ِى  إَّلْح َّن  َّه ِ ق َّكَّن ُّزَّ ذ َّلِك َّ عَّلَّى مُّوسَّى ' ق َّا َّوْجَّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ إ ِن  َّ مَّا ي ُّعْطِى  عَّلَّى  َّ ق َّا َّعْطِهَّا   مَّا 
سَّا َّلَّث ْ ق َّق َّعَّلَّ ق َّذَّل َّي ْهُّ عَّلَّى ق َّن ْزِ ي ُّوسُّف َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّاسْي َّح ْزَّج َّهُّ مِن ْ س َّاطِى ِ إَّلن ِّي لِ ق ِى  صُّن ْذُّوق ٍ  مَّزْمَّزٍ ق َّلَّم َّا إ َّج ْزَّج َّهُّ 
طَّلَّغَّ إَّلْق َّمَّزُّ ق َّحَّمَّلَّهُّ إ ِلَّى إَّلس  َّامِ ق َّلِذ َّلِك َّ ي َّحْمِلُّ إ َّهْلُّ إَّلْكِي َّاب ِ مَّوْي َّاهُّمْ إ ِ لَّى إَّلس  َّامِ . 
وهو ي وسف  ن ن  ي عق وب  علي هما إلسلام، وما ذ كز إلله عز  وج ل ي وسف  ق ى  إلق زإ ن  ع ي زة .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "The souls are in the form of 
bodies in a tree of Paradise; they converse and recognize one another. When a soul 
joins them, they say, 'Leave it, for it has escaped a great terror.'  
Then they ask it, 'What did such -and-such person do, and what did such -and-such 
person do?'  
If it responds, 'I left them alive,' they have hope for them.  
But if it responds, 'They have died,' they say, 'They have fallen.'".  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 244 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 193 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 
633 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 1, p. 330 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 3, p. 724 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 6, p. 269 
• Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 558 • Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa’iq, Part 9, p. 220 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 593
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٩٣

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said :  
Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, revealed to Musa ibn Imran {a.s} to bring out the bones 
of Yusuf {a.s} from Egypt ’, and promised him the rising of the moon.  
However, the moon delayed in rising so he inquired about someone who knew its 
location. He was told of an elderly woman who knew its whereabouts.  
He summoned her, and a paralyzed, blind elderly woman was brought before him.  
Musa {a.s} asked: 'Do you know the grave of Yusuf {a.s}?'  
She replied: 'Yes.'  
Musa {a.s} said: 'Then inform me of its location.'  
She said: 'I will not do so unless you grant me certain conditions: to restore my legs so 
that I may walk, to return my eyesight, to restore my youth, and to make me with you 
in Paradise.'  
This request was burdensome for Musa {a.s}, but Allah {SWT}, Mighty and Majestic, 
revealed to him,  
'You are only granting these on My behalf, so fulfil her request.'  
So Musa {a.s} did as She asked.  
She then guided him to the grave of Yusuf {a.s}, and Musa {a.s} extracted it from the banks 
of the Nile in a marble coffin. When he brought it out, the moon rose. He then carried 
it to Sham (the Levant). 
For this reason, the People of the Book carry their dead to Sham."  
[AL SADUQ] 
This is the same Yusuf {a.s}, the son of Yaqoob {a.s}, and Allah {SWT} hath not mentioned 
any other ‘Yusuf’ in the Quran other than him. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 193 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 14, p. 1596 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 
55, p. 171 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 2, p. 474 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol. 6, p. 388 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 594
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٩٤

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The largest (in purity) a person is, is on the day they are born, and the smallest (in 
purity) they are, is on the day they die”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 194 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 275 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 595
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٩٥

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, has not created any certainty without doubt more 
resembling doubt without certainty than death”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 194 • Al-Khisal, Part 1, p. 14 • Tahaf al-Uqool, Part 1, p. 
364 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 191 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 1, p. 298 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 6, p. 127 • Bihar 
Al Anwar, Vol. 75, p. 246 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 20, p. 807 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 596
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٩٦

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The first person for whom a bier (funeral carriage) was made was Sayyida Fatimah Al-
Zahra {S.A}, daughter of Muhammad {saws}."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 194 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Part 3, p. 220

hadiths.chapter: 28. THE CHAPTER ON PRAYER AND ITS LIMITS
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s} said: Prayer has four thousand gates (aspects 
or elements)”. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 272 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 195 • Al-Khasal, Part 2, 
p. 638 • Uyūn al-Akhbār, Part 1, p. 255 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 242 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 827 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol. 8, p. 827 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 303 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 28. THE CHAPTER ON PRAYER AND ITS LIMITS
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Prayer has four thousand limits (boundaries or rules)”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 272 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, 
Volume 1, p. 195 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 242 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 827

hadiths.ar:
601 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّ :  ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝ إ ِن  َّ إَّلص َّلاٰة َّ كٰان َّث ْ عَّلَّى إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ كِي ٰان اً مَّوْق ُّوي اً  ۝»   
ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah ibn A'yan said: I asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} to inform 
me of what Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, has made obligatory regarding prayers."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Five prayers in the night and day." 
I asked him, "Did Allah {SWT} name them and clarify them in His Book?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, said to His Prophet :  
'Establish prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night' (Surah Al-Isra 
17:78).  
The decline (duluk) of the sun refers to its noon passing, so from the decline of the sun 
until the darkness of the night, there are four prayers (Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib and Isha) 
that Allah {SWT} has named and specified their times. The darkness of the night is its 
midpoint.  
Then Allah {SWT} said: "And the recitation at dawn, for the recitation at dawn is witnessed 
by angels˺." (Surah Al-Isra 17:78) - This is the fifth prayer.  
Allah {SWT} said regarding this: "Establish prayer ˹O’ Prophet˺ at both ends of the day and 
in the early part of the night ." (Surah Hud 11:114) - and its two ends are Maghrib 
(sunset) and Fajr (dawn). 
"And during parts of the night," (Surah Hud 11:114) which refers to the Isha prayer.  
Allah {SWT} also said: " Guard the prayers and especially the middle prayer ," (Surah Al -
Baqarah 2:238) - which is the noon (Dhuhr) prayer, and this is the first prayer that the 
Messenger of Allah {saws} performed. It is between two daytime prayers: the Fajr prayer 
and the Asr prayer." 
He continued: "In some recitations, it reads 'Maintain the prayers and the middle 
prayer and the Asr prayer' (Surah Al Baqarah 2:238). Stand for Allah {SWT} with devotion 
during the middle prayer."  
This verse was revealed on a Jumu’ah when the Messenger of Allah {saws} was traveling, 
and he performed Qunoot in it, keeping it in both travel and residency, and added two 
units for non-traveler.  
The two units added by the Prophet on Jumu’ah for non -travelers are due to the two 
sermons with the Imam. Whoever prays on Jumu’ah without a congregation should 
perform four units as one would perform Dhuhr on other days."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 271 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 195 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 
2, p. 354 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 241 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 35 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 10 • Tafsir Al-
Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 496 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 3, p. 137 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 29, H. 600

hadiths.ar:
602  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  لَّم َّا إ ُّسْزِى َّ ن ِهِ إ َّمَّزَّةُّ رَّن  ُّهُّ ن ِح َّمْسِي ن َّ صَّلاَّة ً ق َّمَّز َّ عَّلَّى إَّلن  َّن ِي ِّي ن َّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ  
لأَّ ي َّسْا َّلُّون َّهُّ عَّن ْ س َّى ْءٍ جَّي  َّى إِن ْي َّهَّى إ ِلَّى مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ ن ِا َّى ِّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّمَّزَّك َّ رَّن  ُّك َّ ق َّق َّال  
صَّلاَّة ً ي ُّم َّ  مَّز َّ ن ِالن  َّن ِي ِّي ن َّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ لأَّ  
ي َّسْا َّلُّون َّهُّ عَّن ْ س َّى ْءٍ جَّي  َّى مَّز َّ ن ِمُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ ن ِا َّى ِّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّمَّزَّك َّ رَّن  ُّك َّ ق َّ ق َّال 
ق َّق َّالَّ إِسْا َّلْ رَّن  َّك َّ إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ ُّم َّي َّك َّ   لأَّ ي ُّطِي ق ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّسَّا َّلَّ رَّن  َّهُّ ق َّحَّظ َّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّس ْزإً ي ُّم َّ مَّز َّ ن ِالن  َّن ِي ِّي ن َّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ لأَّ ي َّسْا َّلُّون َّهُّ 
عَّن ْ س َّى ْءٍ جَّي  َّى مَّز َّ ن ِمُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ ن ِا َّى ِّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّمَّزَّك َّ رَّن  ُّك َّ ق َّق َّال ي ِي ن َّ صَّ لاَّة ً
رَّن  َّك َّ إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ ُّم َّي َّك َّ لأَّ ي ُّطِي ق ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّسَّا َّلَّ رَّن  َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّحَّظ َّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّس ْزإً ي ُّم َّ مَّز َّ  ن ِالن  َّن ِي ِّي ن َّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ لأَّ ي َّسْا َّلُّون َّهُّ 
عَّن ْ س َّى ْءٍ جَّي  َّى مَّز َّ ن ِمُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ  إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ ن ِا َّى ِّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّمَّزَّك َّ رَّن  ُّك َّ ق َّق َّال 
رَّن  َّك َّ إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ ُّم َّي َّك َّ  لأَّ ي ُّطِي ق ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّسَّا َّلَّ رَّن  َّهُّ ق َّحَّظ َّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّس ْزإً ي ُّم َّ مَّز َّ ن ِالن  َّن ِي ِّي ن َّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ لأَّ ي َّسْا َّلُّون َّهُّ عَّن ْ   س َّى ْءٍ  
جَّي  َّى مَّز َّ ن ِمُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ ن ِا َّى ِّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّمَّزَّك َّ رَّن  ُّك َّ ق َّق َّال ب ٍ
إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ ُّم َّي َّك َّ لأَّ ي ُّطِي ق ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّا ِن ِّى  ج ِن ْث ُّ إ ِلَّى ن َّن ِى  إ ِسْزَّإن ِي لَّ ن ِمَّا إِق ْي َّزَّض َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ و  ج َّل َّ عَّلَّي ْهِمْ ق َّلَّمْ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّوإ ن ِهِ 
وَّ لَّمْ ي َّق ِز ُّوإ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّسَّا َّلَّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ رَّن  َّهُّ   عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّح َّق  َّف َّ عَّن ْهُّ ق َّح َّعَّلَّهَّا ج َّمْساً ي ُّم َّ مَّز َّ ن ِالن  َّن ِي ِّي ن َّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ 
ن َّن ِى ٍّ لأَّ ي َّسْا َّلُّون َّهُّ عَّن ْ س َّى ْءٍ جَّي  َّى مَّز َّ ن ِمُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ ن ِا َّى ِّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّ مَّزَّك َّ رَّن  ُّك َّ ق َّق َّال 
صَّلَّوَّإب ٍُّ »إ َّن ْ إ َّعُّوذَّ إ ِلَّى رَّن ِّى 
ق َّح َّاءَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن ِح َّمْسِ صَّلَّوَّإب ٍُّ وَّق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ 
ن ْن َّ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّن ْ إ ُّم َّي ِى  ج َّي ْزإً وَّق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said regarding the words of Allah {SWT},: 
"Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers a timed decree "  
(Surah An-Nisa 4:103). 
Imam {a.s} said: "It means obligatory (prescribed)”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Da‘ā’im al-Islām, Part 1, p. 131 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 40 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 8 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 25 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 545 • Tafseer 
Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 3, p. 530 
﴿HADITH ﴾
